reasoning: Lilly King set the 100m breaststroke long course (50m) world record of 1:04.13 in July 2017. Since the knowledge cutoff is late October 2025 and she still holds the record, the remaining period (November 2025 to January 2026) falls during the traditional short course (25m pool) season. Elite swimmers rarely peak for long course races during these months, as the major international long course events (Olympics, LC World Championships) run during the Northern Hemisphere summer. While some domestic long course events exist (like the US Open in December), a world record that has stood for over 8 years and is significantly ahead of active competitors' personal bests has an extremely low probability of suddenly falling outside a peak championship window. The very slight progressive reductions in probability reflect baseline uncertainty surrounding regional events or administrative changes.
